ip_outcome_note: Moogle Knight / Mog Knight is a moogle-exclusive job class in Final Fantasy Tactics Advance, not a named individual character. The depicted figure is a generic Mog Knight. IP is Square Enix internal to their own TCG product — no external crossover risk. ip_verified flipped to true; ip_confidence remains high.

trivia_bullets.text: Moogle Knight (8-086C) is a Common card from FFTCG Opus VIII, released March 22, 2019. Opus VIII featured FFTA alongside FFXIV: Stormblood, FFVII, FFIX, and FF Brave Exvius; exclusive art was provided by Ryoma Ito (FFTA artist), Yasuhisa Izumisawa, and Toshitaka Matsuda.
sources: Square Enix / na.finalfantasy.com news/1043, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Earth-element Forward, Cost 3, Power 5000, job: Standard Unit. Earth-element in FFTCG aligns with physicality and ground-bound power, consistent with the Mog Knight's stat archetype in FFTA.
sources: TCGPlayer / Collector's Cache retailer listings, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: IP clarification: Moogle Knight (also Mog Knight in FFTA) is a race-exclusive job class, not a named character. Highest-attack moogle class in FFTA; Paladin-comparable stats; signature abilities: Mog Rush, Mog Guard, Ultima Charge, Charge Combo. The depicted figure is a generic Mog Knight.
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki: Mog Knight; StrategyWiki: FFTA/Mog_Knight, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The Mog Knight is locked to the Moogle race in FFTA and requires only one mastered Animist ability to unlock — an accessible early-game power class. Race-locked jobs are a defining FFTA design feature vs. race-agnostic jobs in other FF titles.
sources: GameFAQs FFTA Moogle Jobs Guide; StrategyWiki FFTA/Mog_Knight, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Moogles are one of the most consistent recurring races across the Final Fantasy series (FFIII through FFXVI). This card specifically anchors the Mog Knight iteration from the GBA-era Tactics branch within the FFTCG corpus.
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki: Moogle, via WebSearch snippet
